description: High-energy power rock anthem with driving guitars, thundering drums, and a stadium-sized chorus
suno_tags: power rock, electric guitar, driving drums, anthemic chorus, male vocals, stadium rock, energetic, 130 BPM
vocal_style: Gritty and powerful with raspy edge. Verses are restrained and building, pre-chorus pushes upward with urgency, chorus is full-throated and soaring. Think fist-in-the-air delivery that fills an arena.

tips: Write for 50,000 people to scream back at you — simple, powerful words
Chorus should work as a chant — short words, strong consonants
Verses tell the struggle, chorus is the battle cry
Use fire, storm, lightning, fight imagery — primal and elemental
Bridge should drop energy before the guitar solo builds it back

example_lyrics:
[Intro]
[Electric Guitar]
[Instrumental]

[Verse 1]
They said the fire was dying
That we were running out of road
But I've got thunder in my chest
And a story left to be told

[Pre-Chorus]
So light the match and watch it burn
We've got nothing left to lose

[Chorus]
[Powerful]
We are the ones who never break
Standing tall through the earthquake
Scream it loud, let the world shake
We are the ones who never break

[Verse 2]
Scars like medals on my skin
Every wound a lesson learned
They tried to build a cage around us
But we set the walls to burn

[Pre-Chorus]
So raise your voice and hold the line
This is our moment, this is our time

[Chorus]
[Powerful]
We are the ones who never break
Standing tall through the earthquake
Scream it loud, let the world shake
We are the ones who never break

[Bridge]
[Soft]
In the silence before the storm
When the doubt creeps in at night
Remember every scar you carry
Is proof you survived the fight

[Guitar Solo]
[Electric Guitar]
[Instrumental]

[Chorus]
[Belted]
We are the ones who never break
Standing tall through the earthquake
Scream it loud, let the world shake
We are the ones who never break

[Chorus]
We are the ones who never break

[Outro]
[Instrumental]
[End]
do: Double the final chorus for maximum anthemic impact
Include a guitar solo section — it's essential for rock anthems
Drop the energy in the bridge to create contrast before the final explosion
Use 'we' and 'us' language — anthems are communal, not individual
Front-load the chorus with the strongest, most chantable line
dont: Don't write quiet or introspective lyrics for the chorus
Don't skip the guitar solo — rock fans expect it
Don't use electronic or synth-heavy production cues
Don't make verses too long — 4 lines, then move
Don't end softly — rock anthems finish with full power or a final hit
